questions.prompt_text: An AI agent operating in a Retail Inventory Management system processes 28 tasks per minute. How many tasks will it complete in 8 hours, assuming zero downtime?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Convert the time window from hours to minutes: 8 hours × 60 minutes/hour = 480 minutes. Step 2: Multiply by the task rate: 28 tasks/minute × 480 minutes = 13,440 tasks.

questions.prompt_text: In a Financial Fraud Detection agentic pipeline, Tool A costs $0.23 per call and achieves 72% accuracy. Tool B costs $0.48 per call and achieves 80% accuracy. If the pipeline makes 3572 calls, what is the cost-per-accurate-result for each tool, and which tool is more cost-efficient?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Tool A — Step 1: Accurate results = 3572 × (72 / 100) = 3572 × 0.72 = 2,571.84. Step 2: Total cost = 3572 × $0.23 = $821.56. Step 3: Cost per accurate result = $821.56 / 2,571.84 ≈ $0.3194. Tool B — Step 1: Accurate results = 3572 × (80 / 100) = 3572 × 0.80 = 2,857.60. Step 2: Total cost = 3572 × $0.48 = $1,714.56. Step 3: Cost per accurate result = $1,714.56 / 2,857.60 = $0.60. Comparison: Tool A ($0.3194 per accurate result) < Tool B ($0.60 per accurate result), so Tool A is more cost-efficient.
pre_computed_answer.final_result: Tool A: ≈ $0.3194 per accurate result; Tool B: $0.60 per accurate result. Tool A is more cost-efficient.

questions.prompt_text: A News Summarization Pipeline workflow requires 3 agents to each complete a sub-task. Each agent takes 12.9 seconds. If run sequentially, what is the total latency? If run in parallel (ignoring overhead), what is the total latency? What is the time saved by parallelization?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Sequential latency = NUM_AGENTS × AGENT_LATENCY_SECONDS = 3 × 12.9 = 38.7 seconds. Step 2: Parallel latency = AGENT_LATENCY_SECONDS = 12.9 seconds (all agents run simultaneously). Step 3: Time saved = 38.7 − 12.9 = 25.8 seconds. Step 4: Speedup factor = 38.7 / 12.9 = 3×.
pre_computed_answer.final_result: Sequential latency: 38.7 seconds; Parallel latency: 12.9 seconds; Time saved: 25.8 seconds (3× speedup)

questions.prompt_text: A Hospital Emergency Triage RAG agent has a knowledge base of 1969 documents. A similarity threshold of 0.73 (on a 0–1 scale) filters retrieved results. In a test run, 577 documents exceeded the threshold. What percentage of the knowledge base was retrieved? If each retrieved document costs $0.006 to process, what is the total retrieval cost?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Retrieval percentage = (RETRIEVED_DOCS / TOTAL_DOCUMENTS) × 100 = (577 / 1969) × 100 ≈ 29.30%. Step 2: Total retrieval cost = RETRIEVED_DOCS × COST_PER_DOC = 577 × $0.006 = $3.462.

questions.prompt_text: An Autonomous Vehicle Navigation agentic pipeline has 4 sequential steps. Each step has an independent success rate of 90%. What is the probability (as a percentage) that the entire pipeline completes without error? If 221 independent runs are executed, how many successful end-to-end completions are expected?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Convert success rate to decimal: 90 / 100 = 0.90. Step 2: Pipeline success probability = 0.90^4 = 0.9 × 0.9 × 0.9 × 0.9 = 0.6561. Step 3: Pipeline success percentage = 0.6561 × 100 = 65.61%. Step 4: Expected successful runs = NUM_RUNS × pipeline_success_probability = 221 × 0.6561 ≈ 144.998, which rounds to approximately 145 runs.
pre_computed_answer.final_result: Pipeline success probability: 65.61%; Expected successful completions: ≈ 145.00 runs
